title: NOT_FOUND_COLUMN_IN_BLOCK when performing grouped select queries on tables that have row policies applied
description:
### Company or project name
[PayNow](https://paynow.gg)
### Describe what's wrong
The following error message is returned when attempting to perform a grouped select query on a table with a row policy applied.
Received exception from server (version 25.11.2):
Code: 10. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Not found column deleted in block. There are only columns: age. (NOT_FOUND_COLUMN_IN_BLOCK)
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/45fcc30f-6f46-424e-bb10-c740ee3d043d
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Version 25.11.2.24
Create a replacing merge tree with the version and deleted parameters configured, insert sample data, apply a row policy that only permits the visibility of rows that are not deleted and then simply perform a grouped select query.
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/45fcc30f-6f46-424e-bb10-c740ee3d043d
```
CREATE TABLE users (
uid Int16,
name String,
age Int16,
deleted UInt8,
version UInt64,
) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree(version, deleted)
PRIMARY KEY uid
ORDER BY uid;
INSERT INTO users VALUES (1231, 'John', 33, 0, 1);
INSERT INTO users VALUES (6666, 'Ksenia', 48, 0, 1);
INSERT INTO users VALUES (8888, 'Alice', 20, 0, 1);
INSERT INTO users VALUES (1010, 'Jack', 50, 0, 1);
INSERT INTO users VALUES (8288, 'Peter', 37, 0, 1);
INSERT INTO users VALUES (8818, 'Sarah', 39, 0, 1);
CREATE ROW POLICY only_male ON users USING deleted = 0 TO ALL;
-- Broken
SELECT age
FROM users FINAL
WHERE age > 30 AND age < 40
GROUP BY age;
-- Working
-- SELECT age
-- FROM users
-- WHERE age > 30 AND age < 40
-- GROUP BY age;
```
### Expected behavior
Return the data, not an error.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Received exception from server (version 25.11.2):
Code: 10. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Not found column deleted in block. There are only columns: age. (NOT_FOUND_COLUMN_IN_BLOCK)
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users_extended (uid INT, name VARCHAR(255), age SMALLINT, deleted TINYINT, version BIGINT, salary FLOAT) ENGINE=REPLACE INTO users_extended (uid, name, age, deleted, version, salary) PRIMARY KEY (uid);
CREATE ROW POLICY only_females ON users_extended USING (deleted = 0 AND name NOT LIKE 'J%') TO ALL;
SELECT AVG(salary) FROM users_extended WHERE age > 30 GROUP BY age;
key_bug_features: Grouped select query on table with row policy applied
